message.content:
You are confirmer #2, independent. Confirm this fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (the named test DISCRIMINATES — fails if reverted). Read the actual changed file + test at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.
Fix CV-O-1 in scripts/capture_transcripts.py
What: _copy_scrubbed scrubs EVERY text file (decode-strict; binary copied verbatim), not only .jsonl — closes the .json workflow-transcript PII/secret bypass. Working tree re-scrubbed by the pre-commit hook (0 raw-username occurrences); git history needs filter-repo before publish (documented).
Test(s): test_copy_scrubbed_scrubs_non_jsonl_text_files, test_copy_scrubbed_copies_binary_verbatim
Verdict CONFIRMED or CONCERN; be specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Capture the active Claude session's JSONL transcript(s) into the repo for provenance.
3	
4	Run by the pre-commit hook (.githooks/pre-commit) so every commit pulls in an up-to-date
5	snapshot of the agent interactions/effort behind it — an auditable record alongside the work.
6	
7	What it captures: the *active* session (the most-recently-modified top-level transcript in the
8	Claude project dir) plus that session's ``<id>/`` subtree (subagents + workflow transcripts).
9	Other/unrelated sessions are left out. Credential-shaped secrets are redacted on the way in.
10	
11	Honest scope: transcripts still contain local filesystem paths and may contain the operator's
12	email and internal reasoning. If this repo is published, review/exclude ``transcripts/`` first
13	(see transcripts/README.md). The wheel never ships them (only ``src/assay_engine`` is packaged).
14	
15	Config: ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC overrides the source dir. Exits 0 on any error (never blocks a
16	commit).
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import datetime as _dt
22	import hashlib
23	import json
24	import os
25	import re
26	import sys
27	from pathlib import Path
28	
29	DEFAULT_SRC = Path.home() / ".claude" / "projects" / "-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc"
30	DEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "sessions"
31	MANIFEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "MANIFEST.json"
32	
33	# Credential-shaped secrets to redact (defense). Pass 3 (#F-024) extends this to operator PII —
34	# email addresses and the home-directory username embedded in filesystem paths — so committing
35	# transcripts as provenance (the campaign mandate) does not also publish the operator's identity.
36	# Reasoning content and the path STRUCTURE remain (legitimate provenance); see the README caveat
37	# for the full public-release consideration.
38	_SECRET_PATTERNS = [
39	    re.compile(r"sk-ant-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2,}"),  # any sk-ant- token incl. oat01 OAuth + placeholders
40	    re.compile(r"\bgh[posru]_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}\b"),  # GitHub tokens
41	    re.compile(r"\bAKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}\b"),  # AWS access key id
42	    # bearer tokens incl. base64url/base64 JWTs — must cover + / = or the token is split at the
43	    # first such char and only the leading fragment is redacted (pass 3, #F-051).
44	    re.compile(r"(?i)bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-+/=]{20,}"),
45	    # explicit OAuth/token env values: "...OAUTH_TOKEN":"<value>" / ...KEY=<value>
46	    re.compile(r'((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)"\s*:\s*")[^"]{8,}(")'),
47	    re.compile(r"((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)=)[^\s\"']{8,}"),
48	    # operator PII (#F-024): email addresses, and the username segment of a home path
49	    # (/Users/[REDACTED] /home/[REDACTED] — the path STRUCTURE is kept, only the identity is masked.
50	    re.compile(r"\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}\b"),  # email → [REDACTED]
51	    re.compile(r"(/(?:Users|home)/)[^/\s\"']+"),  # /Users/[REDACTED] → /Users/[REDACTED]    # The Claude project-dir SLUG (e.g. -Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc): redact the username segment
53	    # regardless of length (#H-018) — the bare-token rule below skips short handles to avoid
54	    # over-redacting common words, but the slug form is unambiguous PII at any length.
55	    re.compile(r"(-(?:Users|home)-)[^-\s/\"']+"),  # -Users-[REDACTED]- → -Users-[REDACTED]-
56	]
57	
58	
59	def _username_pattern() -> re.Pattern[str] | None:
60	    """A redaction for the bare operator username wherever it appears (#G-007).
61	
62	    The /Users|/home path rule only masks the username inside a path; the same handle leaks via
63	    git author strings, prose, and the Claude project-dir slug (``-Users-[REDACTED]-...``). Derive the
64	    handle from the home directory and redact it as a standalone token too. Skipped for trivially
65	    short/None handles (e.g. a CI 'root') to avoid over-redacting common words.
66	    """
67	    try:
68	        name = Path.home().name
69	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - never break capture over a missing home
70	        return None
71	    if not name or len(name) < 4:
72	        return None
73	    return re.compile(re.escape(name))
74	
75	
76	_USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()
77	
78	
79	def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
80	    for pat in _SECRET_PATTERNS:
81	        if pat.groups == 2:
82	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]\2", text)
83	        elif pat.groups == 1:
84	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]", text)
85	        else:
86	            text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
87	    # Redact the bare operator username everywhere (#G-007), after the path rule so masked paths
88	    # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
89	    if _USERNAME_RE is not None:
90	        text = _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
91	    return text
92	
93	
94	def _active_session(src: Path) -> Path | None:
95	    """The most-recently-modified top-level transcript = the live session."""
96	    candidates = sorted(src.glob("*.jsonl"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
97	    return candidates[0] if candidates else None
98	
99	
100	def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
101	    try:
102	        dstf.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
103	        raw = srcf.read_bytes()
104	        # Scrub EVERY text file, not only .jsonl (#CV-O-1): the session subtree also holds .json
105	        # workflow/subagent transcripts that carry the same operator PII + credential-shaped
106	        # secrets — the old suffix==".jsonl" gate copied those RAW, bypassing redaction entirely.
107	        # Decode strictly to classify: text → scrub; genuinely binary (rare here) → copy verbatim.
108	        try:
109	            text = raw.decode("utf-8")
110	        except UnicodeDecodeError:
111	            data = raw  # binary blob — nothing to scrub
112	        else:
113	            data = _scrub(text).encode("utf-8")
114	        if dstf.exists() and dstf.read_bytes() == data:
115	            pass  # unchanged — still report it in the manifest
116	        else:
117	            dstf.write_bytes(data)
118	        return {
119	            "path": str(dstf.relative_to(DEST.parent.parent)),
120	            "bytes": len(data),
121	            "sha256": hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest(),
122	        }
123	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — provenance capture must never break a commit
124	        return None
125	
126	
127	def main() -> int:
128	    src = Path(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC", str(DEFAULT_SRC)))
129	    if not src.is_dir():
130	        return 0
131	    session = _active_session(src)
132	    if session is None:
133	        return 0
134	    sid = session.stem
135	    captured: list[dict] = []
136	
137	    rec = _copy_scrubbed(session, DEST / f"{sid}.jsonl")
138	    if rec:
139	        captured.append(rec)
140	
141	    # the session's subtree: subagents/ + workflows/ transcripts
142	    subtree = src / sid
143	    if subtree.is_dir():
144	        for f in sorted(subtree.rglob("*")):
145	            if f.is_file():
146	                rel = f.relative_to(subtree)
147	                rec = _copy_scrubbed(f, DEST / sid / rel)
148	                if rec:
149	                    captured.append(rec)
150	
151	    try:
152	        MANIFEST.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
153	        MANIFEST.write_text(
154	            json.dumps(
155	                {
156	                    "captured_at": _dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
157	                    "active_session": sid,
158	                    "n_files": len(captured),
159	                    "total_bytes": sum(c["bytes"] for c in captured),
160	                    "files": captured,
161	                },
162	                indent=2,
163	            )
164	            + "\n",
165	            encoding="utf-8",
166	        )
167	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
168	        return 0
169	    print(f"captured {len(captured)} transcript file(s) for session {sid}")
170	    return 0
171	
172	
173	if __name__ == "__main__":
174	    sys.exit(main())
175

content.text: Now let me find the tests.

content.content:
1	"""Guards for repo scripts + examples (pass 3: #F-051, #F-048).
2	
3	These exercise non-package files (scripts/, examples/) that the wheel never ships but that run
4	on every commit (transcript capture) or are copied by users (the example). Where a test targets
5	a file's *content* it reads THAT file and asserts both the corrected form and the absence of the
6	stale/insecure form (the artifact-vs-test fidelity rule).
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import importlib.util
12	from pathlib import Path
13	
14	_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
15	
16	
17	def _load(rel: str, name: str):
18	    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(name, _ROOT / rel)
19	    assert spec and spec.loader
20	    mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
21	    spec.loader.exec_module(mod)
22	    return mod
23	
24	
25	def test_bearer_token_scrub_covers_base64_chars():
26	    # #F-051: a bearer token containing base64 chars (+ / =) must be FULLY redacted, not split at
27	    # the first such char leaving a trailing fragment in the transcript.
28	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts")
29	    text = "Authorization: [REDACTED]"
30	    scrubbed = cap._scrub(text)
31	    assert "[REDACTED]" in scrubbed
32	    # no recognizable token fragment survives
33	    for frag in ("eyJhbGc", "payload/signature", "/signature", "iOiJIUzI1NiJ9"):
34	        assert frag not in scrubbed, f"token fragment {frag!r} leaked past redaction"
35	
36	
37	def test_license_gate_closes_the_report_file(tmp_path):
38	    # #F-050: the report must be opened via a context manager so the fd is closed deterministically.
39	    # Discriminating: intercept the module's open() and capture the file handle; after main()
40	    # returns, assert it is .closed. A bare json.load(open(...)) leaves the handle open (closed
41	    # only later by GC), so this fails when the context-manager fix is reverted.
42	    import builtins
43	    import json
44	
45	    gate = _load("scripts/license_gate.py", "license_gate_fd")
46	    p = tmp_path / "licenses.json"
47	    p.write_text(json.dumps([{"Name": "ok", "Version": "1", "License": "MIT"}]), encoding="utf-8")
48	
49	    handles = []
50	    real_open = builtins.open
51	
52	    def tracking_open(*a, **k):
53	        fh = real_open(*a, **k)
54	        handles.append(fh)
55	        return fh
56	
57	    gate.open = tracking_open  # module globals win over builtins for the bare open() call
58	    rc = gate.main(str(p))
59	    assert rc == 0
60	    assert handles and all(h.closed for h in handles), (
61	        "license_gate left the report file open (#F-050)"
62	    )
63	
64	
65	def test_transcript_scrub_redacts_operator_pii():
66	    # #F-024 (mandate-compatible mitigation): transcripts are committed as provenance per the
67	    # campaign mandate, but the capture scrubber must mask operator PII — email addresses and the
68	    # home-path username — so identity is not published. The path STRUCTURE is preserved.
69	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_pii")
70	    out = cap._scrub("user [REDACTED] at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/x.py and /home/[REDACTED]/y")
71	    assert "[REDACTED]" not in out
72	    assert "somebody" not in out
73	    assert "/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/x.py" in out  # structure kept, username masked
74	    assert "/home/[REDACTED]/y" in out
75	
76	
77	def test_transcript_scrub_redacts_bare_operator_username():
78	    # #G-007: the operator username must be redacted wherever it appears (git author strings,
79	    # project-dir slugs like -Users-[REDACTED]-..., prose), not only inside /Users|/home paths.
80	    from pathlib import Path
81	
82	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_user")
83	    name = Path.home().name
84	    if not name or len(name) < 4:
85	        import pytest
86	
87	        pytest.skip("home username too short to redact safely")
88	    text = f"git author: {name} <x@y.z>; project dir -Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc"
89	    out = cap._scrub(text)
90	    assert name not in out  # bare handle masked everywhere, not only in a /Users path
91	
92	
93	def test_transcript_scrub_redacts_username_in_project_dir_slug():
94	    # #H-018: the Claude project-dir slug (-Users-[REDACTED]-...) must have its username redacted at
95	    # ANY length (the bare-token rule skips short handles to avoid over-redaction, but the slug
96	    # form is unambiguous PII).
97	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_slug")
98	    out = cap._scrub("dir: -Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc and -home-[REDACTED]-proj")  # short names
99	    assert "-Users-[REDACTED]-" not in out and "-home-[REDACTED]-" not in out
100	    assert "-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc" in out and "-home-[REDACTED]-proj" in out
101	
102	
103	def test_copy_scrubbed_scrubs_non_jsonl_text_files(tmp_path):
104	    # #CV-O-1: the session subtree holds .json workflow/subagent transcripts; redaction must apply
105	    # to EVERY text file, not only .jsonl, or operator PII + secrets leak into committed files.
106	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_nonjsonl")
107	    src = tmp_path / "wf.json"  # a .json (NOT .jsonl) workflow transcript
108	    src.write_text('{"path": "/Users/[REDACTED]/x", "tok": "[REDACTED]"}', "utf-8")
109	    dst = tmp_path / "out" / "wf.json"
110	    cap._copy_scrubbed(src, dst)
111	    out = dst.read_text("utf-8")
112	    assert "somebody" not in out and "/Users/[REDACTED]/x" in out  # path username scrubbed
113	    assert "abcdefghijklmnopqrstuv" not in out  # bearer token scrubbed
114	
115	
116	def test_copy_scrubbed_copies_binary_verbatim(tmp_path):
117	    # #CV-O-1: a genuinely binary file (undecodable) is copied unchanged, not corrupted.
118	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_bin")
119	    src = tmp_path / "blob.bin"
120	    src.write_bytes(b"\xff\xfe\x00\x01binary")
121	    dst = tmp_path / "out" / "blob.bin"
122	    cap._copy_scrubbed(src, dst)
123	    assert dst.read_bytes() == b"\xff\xfe\x00\x01binary"
124	
125	
126	def test_example_uses_no_hardcoded_hmac_secret():
127	    # #F-048: the example must not carry a shippable, publicly-known HMAC secret a user could copy
128	    # into production. It derives a fresh random secret per run instead.
129	    src = (_ROOT / "examples" / "minimal_study.py").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
130	    assert b"example-study-secret-key".decode() not in src  # the stale weak literal is gone
131	    assert "os.urandom(32)" in src  # the fresh-per-run secret is in place
132

tmp_path = PosixPath('/private/var/folders/1k/1h7hkdqj69g1w8gb24s99p800000gn/T/pytest-of-[REDACTED]/pytest-544/test_copy_scrubbed_scrubs_non_0')

    def test_copy_scrubbed_scrubs_non_jsonl_text_files(tmp_path):
        # #CV-O-1: the session subtree holds .json workflow/subagent transcripts; redaction must apply
        # to EVERY text file, not only .jsonl, or operator PII + secrets leak into committed files.
        cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_nonjsonl")
        src = tmp_path / "wf.json"  # a .json (NOT .jsonl) workflow transcript
        src.write_text('{"path": "/Users/[REDACTED]/x", "tok": "[REDACTED]"}', "utf-8")
        dst = tmp_path / "out" / "wf.json"
